$metadata.name: FaberLoom Base Tokens
$metadata.author: FaberLoom Design System
$metadata.description: Primitive design tokens for the FaberLoom B2B agentic platform. All semantic and component tokens reference these primitives.

color.$description: Color primitives organized by hue. Each ramp follows perceptually uniform steps with 500 as the base color.

cream.$description: Warm paper-kraft background family. Inspired by physical craft paper — never cold, always inviting.

50.$description: Near-white with imperceptible cream warmth. For highest emphasis surfaces.

100.$description: Very light cream. Card backgrounds in light mode.

200.$description: Light cream. Hover states on cream surfaces.

500.$description: Base cream. Primary page background — the FaberLoom signature warmth.

600.$description: Dimmed cream. Borders on cream backgrounds.

700.$description: Shadow cream. Muted UI elements on light surfaces.

800.$description: Deep shadow cream. Placeholder text on cream.

900.$description: Dark cream-gray. Disabled states on light backgrounds.

ink.$description: Near-black family with warm olive undertone. Primary text and dark surfaces. Never pure #000.

100.$description: Light warm gray. Borders in dark mode.

200.$description: Medium-light warm gray. Muted borders dark mode.

300.$description: Mid warm gray. Secondary text dark mode.

400.$description: Neutral warm gray. Muted text dark mode.

500.$description: Base ink. Primary text in light mode — warm near-black.

700.$description: Deep ink. Card backgrounds in dark mode.

coral.$description: Single accent interaction color. Buttons, links, active states. Warm terracotta — energetic but grounded.

100.$description: Light coral. Hover/selected backgrounds for accent items.

500.$description: Base coral. Primary action color — buttons, links, focus rings.

700.$description: Dark coral. Text on light coral backgrounds.

900.$description: Cocoa coral. Highest contrast needs on light surfaces.

pizarra.$description: Slate blue-gray. Secondary information, metadata, subtitles, deactivated elements. Cooler complement to warm cream/coral.

50.$description: Slate mist. Very subtle background tint.

300.$description: Medium slate. Muted borders in light mode.

500.$description: Base pizarra. Secondary text, metadata, subtitles.

700.$description: Dark slate. Emphasis text on light backgrounds.

neutral.$description: Warm gray scale from white to black. Tinted slightly toward cream/olive — never cold blue-gray. Use for chrome, dividers, disabled states.

0.$description: Pure white. Overlays, highest emphasis text on dark.

50.$description: Warm white. Slight cream tint for seamless blending with cream-500.

100.$description: Very light warm gray. Subtle backgrounds.

200.$description: Light warm gray. Disabled backgrounds, readonly inputs.

300.$description: Light-medium warm gray. Borders in light mode.

500.$description: Mid warm gray. Muted icons, disabled text.

800.$description: Very dark warm gray. Strong secondary text.

1000.$description: Deepest warm black. Neutral overlay base.

functional.$description: Semantic functional colors for feedback states. Each has its own ramp to ensure WCAG compliance across all surface combinations.

success.$description: Positive completion — agent task done, validation passed, item saved. Muted sage green, harmonious with warm palette.

50.$description: Success mist. Background tint for success states.

200.$description: Soft success. Success borders, subtle highlights.

500.$description: Base success. Primary success indicator color.

700.$description: Dark success. Success text on light backgrounds.

800.$description: Deep success. Strong emphasis success text.

900.$description: Cocoa success. Maximum contrast on cream.

warning.$description: Attention needed — agent waiting, deprecated feature, non-blocking issue. Warm amber, distinct from coral accent.

50.$description: Warning mist. Background tint for warning states.

500.$description: Base warning. Primary warning indicator color.

700.$description: Dark warning. Warning text on light backgrounds.

800.$description: Deep warning. Strong emphasis warning text.

900.$description: Cocoa warning. Maximum contrast on cream.

danger.$description: Critical failure — agent error, deletion, blocking issue. Brick red, clearly distinct from coral accent (which is orange-red).

50.$description: Danger mist. Background tint for error states.

500.$description: Base danger. Primary error indicator color.

700.$description: Dark danger. Error text on light backgrounds.

800.$description: Deep danger. Strong emphasis error text.

900.$description: Cocoa danger. Maximum contrast on cream.

info.$description: Informational — tips, progress updates, neutral notices. Steel blue, distinct from pizarra slate.

50.$description: Info mist. Background tint for info states.

500.$description: Base info. Primary info indicator color.

700.$description: Dark info. Info text on light backgrounds.

800.$description: Deep info. Strong emphasis info text.

900.$description: Cocoa info. Maximum contrast on cream.

agent.$description: Agent state colors — foreground/background pairs for the 6 agent lifecycle states. Each state has bg, fg, border, and icon tokens.

idle.$description: Agent available, not processing. Calm blue-gray suggesting readiness.

bg.$description: Idle background. Subtle blue-gray tint on cream.

fg.$description: Idle foreground text. Dark blue-gray for WCAG AA on bg.

border.$description: Idle border. Medium blue-gray for outlines.

running.$description: Agent actively processing. Energetic coral — the FaberLoom accent in motion.

fg.$description: Running foreground. Deep coral for WCAG AA on bg.

waiting.$description: Agent waiting for user input (HITL). Warm amber demanding attention without urgency.

fg.$description: Waiting foreground. Dark amber-brown for WCAG AA on bg.

success.$description: Task completed successfully. Confident sage green.

fg.$description: Success foreground. Deep sage for WCAG AA on bg.

error.$description: Task failed, needs attention. Distinctive brick red.

fg.$description: Error foreground. Deep brick for WCAG AA on bg.

blocked.$description: Blocked by policy, trust boundary, or safety check. Muted purple-gray suggesting suspension.

fg.$description: Blocked foreground. Dark warm gray for WCAG AA on bg.

space.$description: 4-point grid spacing scale. All UI spacing derives from this base unit for rhythm and consistency.

0.$description: Zero spacing. Collapsed gaps, overlaid elements.

1.$description: Micro spacing. Icon gaps, tight internal padding.

2.$description: Extra small. Tight component internal spacing.

3.$description: Small. Default gap between related elements.

4.$description: Base spacing. Default padding inside components.

6.$description: Medium. Card internal padding, section gaps.

radius.$description: Border radius scale. FaberLoom uses restrained rounding — professional, not playful.

0.$description: No radius. Structural dividers, full-bleed sections.

sm.$description: Subtle rounding. Minimal softening for data-dense UIs.

base.$description: Base radius. Default for buttons, inputs, compact cards.

xl.$description: Extra large. Modals, dialogs, feature cards.

full.$description: Fully rounded. Pills, badges, avatar circles.

typography.$description: Type scale for FaberLoom. Display uses Crimson Pro Italic for editorial elegance; UI uses Inter for clarity; Mono uses IBM Plex Mono for data.

display.$description: Display typeface. Italic 500 for headings, hero text, quotes.

ui.$description: UI typeface. 300-800 weight range for all interface text.

mono.$description: Monospace typeface. Code, timestamps, agent logs, JSON display.

light.$description: Light weight. Large display text, hero paragraphs.

medium.$description: Medium weight. Subheadings, emphasis within body.

semibold.$description: Semibold. Section headings, card titles, active nav.

extrabold.$description: Extra bold. Maximum emphasis, display numbers.

size.$description: Type size scale with line-height and letter-spacing. All rem values assume 16px root.

none.$description: No leading. Display headings, single-line elements.

tight.$description: Tight leading. Headings, compact text blocks.

loose.$description: Loose leading. Maximum readability, accessibility mode.

widest.$description: Widest tracking. Accent labels, minimal text.

shadow.$description: Elevation scale using layered box-shadows. Shadows are warm-tinted (subtle brown, never cold blue-gray) to match the kraft paper aesthetic.

0.$description: No shadow. Flat element at base level.

1.$description: Subtle lift. Interactive items at rest — buttons, inputs.

2.$description: Soft elevation. Cards at rest, dropdowns.

3.$description: Medium elevation. Raised cards, popovers, sticky headers.

4.$description: High elevation. Modals, dialogs, date pickers.

5.$description: Maximum elevation. Full-screen overlays, command palettes, critical dialogs.

dark.$description: Shadow variants optimized for dark mode — softer, less opaque since dark surfaces absorb more shadow.

motion.$description: Temporal primitives for animations and transitions. FaberLoom uses restrained, purposeful motion.

instant.$description: No duration. Immediate state change for reduced-motion.

fast.$description: Fast transition. Hover states, color changes, opacity fades.

base.$description: Base duration. Standard UI transitions — buttons, inputs, toggles.

medium.$description: Medium transition. Dropdowns, tooltips, small panels.

slower.$description: Slower transition. Page transitions, complex animations.

slowest.$description: Slowest transition. Hero animations, onboarding reveals.

linear.$description: Linear. Constant speed for opacity/color fades.

ease-in.$description: Ease in. Accelerating — for elements exiting.

ease-out.$description: Ease out. Decelerating — for elements entering.

ease-in-out.$description: Ease in-out. Smooth symmetric — for modal transitions.

spring.$description: Spring. Slight overshoot — for playful micro-interactions (toggle, badge pop).

opacity.$description: Standardized opacity values for disabled states, overlays, and layered transparency.

z-index.$description: Stacking context primitives. Use sparingly — prefer DOM order where possible.
